step_by_step_thinking: I agree with agent1's assessment. Herpes zoster typically affects only one side of the body due to involvement along nerve roots, making option A "Unilateral occurrence" the primary differentiating feature. This unique pattern helps clinicians quickly identify shingles even if they have similar initial symptoms to other conditions such as impetigo where lesions may appear more widespread without following specific neural pathways.
raw_response:

Herpes zoster typically affects only one side of the body due to involvement along nerve roots, making option A "Unilateral occurrence" the primary differentiating feature. This unique pattern helps clinicians quickly identify shingles even if they have similar initial symptoms to other conditions such as impetigo where lesions may appear more widespread without following specific neural pathways.

Answer: A.
